channel.title: 영문대표홈페이지 > Press > Press Releases
channel.description: RSS 주소목록 영문대표홈페이지 > Press > Press Releases

item.title: Korean Government Provides Humanitarian Assistance to Venezuela Following Earthquake
item.content:encoded: The government of the Republic of Korea has decided to provide Venezuela with USD5 million in humanitarian assistance through international organizations operating on the ground, in order to support the rapid response to and facilitate an early recovery from the recent devastating earthquake in the country that caused a loss of lives and damage to properties. ※ According to the United Nations Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs (OCHA)'s 2026 Global Humanitarian Overview, even prior to the earthquake, Venezuela already had 7.9 million people — more than 25% of its total population — in need of humanitarian assistance, owing to frequent natural disasters such as heavy rains and floodings, as well as political and economic instability including extreme inflation. The government of the Republic of Korea hopes that this assistance will contribute to a swift recovery of the affected areas and to an early restoration of normalcy to daily lives of affected communities.

item.title: Korean Government Provides Additional Humanitarian Assistance to Venezuela Following Earthquake
item.content:encoded: Following its earlier provision of USD 5 million in humanitarian assistance in response to the humanitarian crisis caused by the recent devastating earthquake in Venezuela, the government of the Republic of Korea has decided to provide additional humanitarian assistance worth USD 3.5 million in view of the increasing scale of the damage. Of this additional assistance, USD 3 million will be provided through the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) to support debris removal activities to facilitate the swift return of affected communities to normalcy. In addition, at the request of the government of Venezuela, the government of the Republic of Korea has also decided to provide 30 tons of temporary shelters (family tents), worth approximately USD 500,000, as in-kind assistance to accommodate displaced persons. The government of the Republic of Korea will continue to provide humanitarian assistance to countries and people affected by natural disasters.

item.title: Kim Hee-sang, Former Deputy Minister for Economic Affairs, Appointed as Korea’s New G20 Sherpa
item.content:encoded: Kim Hee-sang, former Deputy Minister for Economic Affairs, has been appointed as Korea’s G20 Sherpa (government representative), succeeding Song In-chang. The G20 Sherpa takes part in negotiations on G20 issues on behalf of the President and leads Korea’s domestic and external preparations for G20 Summits. ※ Kim Hee-sang will assume the role of Korea’s G20 Sherpa on March 14. The government of the Republic of Korea will participate actively in various G20 consultations leading up to the Miami G20 Summit to be held on December 14-15 this year, while continuing preparations for the success of the G20 Summit in Korea in 2028.
